I found the `UsersController.scala` file. Here's the User data model described:

## User Data Model

The `User` is defined as a **case class** with 4 fields:

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|------------|------|-------------|
| `id` | `Int` | The unique identifier for the user |
| `name` | `String` | The user's name |
| `email` | `String` | The user's email address |
| `createdAt` | `java.time.LocalDateTime` | The timestamp when the user was created |

### Code Definition:
```scala
case class User(id: Int, name: String, email: String, createdAt: LocalDateTime)
```

The file also contains sample data with three users:
1. Alice (id: 1, email: alice@example.com, created: 2024-01-15 10:30)
2. Bob (id: 2, email: bob@example.com, created: 2024-02-20 14:00)
3. Charlie (id: 3, email: charlie@example.com, created: 2024-03-05 09:15)
